channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Why the ‘iPhone of smartwatches’ remains so elusive
item.description: Ever since it became obvious that the iPhone was one of the most transformative consumer products in history, a question has been floating out there: Would its impact ever be matched by a device in an even newer category? And if something pulled it off, what would it be? After a decade and a half—so
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: ChatGPT may be making us lonelier
item.description: A new pair of studies from MIT Media Lab and OpenAI found that those who use the chatbot most heavily also experience the most loneliness. The catch-22: it’s unclear whether this is caused by the chatbot itself or if lonely individuals are simply more likely to seek out emotional bonds.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: IKEA's new smart home button with Matter support just leaked –and it looks like my next impulse buy
item.description: A new IKEA smart home switch with Matter support has just leaked –and it should be a versatile dual-button accessory that supports Google Home, Apple HomeKit and Amazon Alexa.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Black Ops 6 Season 3 release date and time in your timezone
item.description: Here's what we know about the Black Ops 6 Season two Reloaded release time, including a look at the new skins and maps that are set to be added.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Musk: Trump will 'go after' people 'pushing the lies' about Tesla
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk suggested Thursday that the Trump administration will crack down on vehicle vandalization, expressing his concerns about protestors targeting Tesla dealerships across the U.S. and abroad. “The president’s made it clear.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Airtel connects India with 100Tbps submarine cable
item.description: In a significant development for digital infrastructure in India, Bharti Airtel has landed the 2Africa Pearls submarine cable, delivering 100 Terabits per second of international network capacity for the country. The submarine cable will connect to Airtel’s cable landing station (CLS) in Mumbai.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: I was already excited for Metroid Prime 4: Beyond, then Retro Studios gave Samus a bullet time mechanic
item.description: Metroid Prime 4: Beyond looks great, but it's Samus's new psychic powers that have me most intrigued.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: La transformación digital, esencial para la supervivencia del sector asegurador
item.description: Los beneficios de la transformación digital ya son palpables para el sector asegurador. Así lo constata el informe Perspectivas de tecnología en seguros 2025: transformando la innovación en ventaja competitiva que firma Concentrix.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows 11’s latest patch declares war on BIOS updates for some Lenovo laptops, blocking them as a security risk in a bizarre turn of events
item.description: Can’t install a BIOS update for your Lenovo laptop? Blame Microsoft’s latest patch for Windows 11 – but fortunately the fix is simple enough.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 6 advanced Gboard tricks for smarter Android typing
item.description: QWERTY, QWERTY, QWERTY. QWERTY. Oh — hi there! Sorry for the slightly nonsensical greeting. I’ve been thinking a lot about keyboards this week, y’see, and how that trusty ol’ QWERTY surface has evolved in our lives. Also, saying “QWERTY” over and over again is surprisingly fun to do.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nueve tendencias de CRM para 2025: la IA redefine la ecuación del cliente
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) tiene el potencial de transformar todos los aspectos de una empresa, pero sobre todo la gestión de las relaciones con los clientes (CRM), las funciones comerciales de cara al exterior de ventas, marketing y servicio al cliente.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The slim phone era is (finally) back
item.description: Smartphones have been around long enough that, to the casual observer, their designs seem to have hit a plateau. And on a functional level, that’s more or less true—we’re all essentially holding the same six-inch-ish rectangle, aside from the occasional foldable exception.
item.dc:creator: Sam Byford, Fast Company

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Adobe announces AI agents for customer interaction
item.description: Adobe has announced Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator, an intelligent reasoning engine that allows AI agents to perform complex decision-making and problem-solving tasks to accelerate customer experience orchestration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AMD gets another downgrade on tough competition with Nvidia
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. is falling further behind Nvidia Corp. in its technological capabilities, a fact that could continue to pressure the stock, according to Jefferies, which downgraded the chipmaker to hold.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Agencies Prepare for Resilience Amid Evolving Risks
item.description: Federal agencies update continuity planning directives to account for evolving risks, including cyber threats and natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Nintendo Virtual Game Card Feature Coming in April
item.description: Nintendo announces a new Switch feature called Virtual Game Card, making it easier to lend out digital games, available in late April and also on Switch 2 at launch.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Commercial innovations to drive FedRAMP’s new approach
item.description: Pete Waterman, the director of the cloud security program known as FedRAMP, said four new community working groups will help give industry a bigger voice. The post Commercial innovations to drive FedRAMP’s new approach first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon allure grows with cheaper shares than Apple, Walmart
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. shares are starting to look like a bargain, a word that has rarely been used to describe the stock.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 big takeaways from Nvidia GTC
item.description: Now that the dust has settled from Nvidia’s GTC 2025, a few industry experts weighed in on some core big picture developments from the conference.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The nation’s statisticians worry about the state of federal data gathering
item.description: One of the government's activities is the gathering of reliable statistics to guide policy and the quality of national statistics has become a concern. The post The nation’s statisticians worry about the state of federal data gathering first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets, although passengers will have to wait until next year until they can take a driverless ride around the nation's capital city.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As federal employees return to office, agencies need a reliable, secure system to meet mandates and deliver efficient, productive facilities
item.description: Federal agencies need a unified platform that can deliver real-time reporting on occupancy, space availability, and opportunities for rightsizing.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Otter.ai’s voice-activated AI agent can answer questions during online meetings
item.description: Otter.ai, which is primarily known for its voice-to-text transcription service, is rolling out a voice-activated AI agent that will participate in and answer questions during online calls.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cosmonic uses WebAssembly to manage apps
item.description: Leveraging the WebAssembly binary instruction format, Cosmonic has launched wasmCloud-based Cosmonic Control, an enterprise control plane for managing distributed applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: In 2025, you can’t have an effective democracy without data literacy
item.description: Data literacy should be a part of the 21st-century civics curriculum. The post In 2025, you can’t have an effective democracy without data literacy first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: A European startup scrubs its attempt to launch an orbital rocket on its first test flight
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A private European aerospace company scrubbed its attempt on Monday to launch the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle from Norway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: World Backup Day is March 31
item.description: Are you ready? World Backup Day is March 31, a day that has been recognized every year since it was first set up in 2011.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Protecting your daughter from deepfakes and online abuse
item.description: Artificial intelligence-generated images and videos that use someone’s likeness typically target women without their consent. The CyberGuy unpacks how to protect loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
